prompt:
You are Agent 2 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-mRoAie.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Implement the email notification adapter fully.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification architecture from the previous session (check src/services/, src/adapters/, src/events/, and any design docs).
2. Implement the email adapter with real logic: template rendering, recipient resolution, send method.
3. Wire the email adapter into the notification service following the established pattern.
4. Add basic unit tests for the email adapter.
5. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Follow the architecture from Session 1 exactly — do not change the notification pattern.
- The email adapter must conform to whatever adapter interface was defined.

parameters.task: Implement email notification adapter with template rendering, recipient resolution, and send method. Wire into existing notification service and add unit tests.

parameters.description: List adapters, events, and services directories

parameters.new_string:
expect(emails[0]?.html).toContain('Thank you for your purchase');
      expect(emails[0]?.body).toContain('Order Confirmation');
parameters.old_string:
expect(emails[0]?.html).toContain('Thank you for your purchase');
      expect(emails[0]?.text).toContain('Order Confirmation');

alternatives.option: Single monolithic send method with inline HTML generation
alternatives.reason_rejected: No separation of concerns — template changes require modifying the adapter, transport is not swappable, harder to test individual parts
Adds a runtime dependency for a small set of templates. The TEMPLATES record is sufficient for 4 notification types and keeps everything in one file.

assumptions: NotificationPayload.recipient may contain either email addresses or user IDs depending on the event handler
Four notification types (order_confirmation, order_status_update, welcome, account_updated) are sufficient for current needs
parameters.context: The EmailAdapter was a stub that stored emails in-memory with no template rendering, recipient resolution, or transport abstraction. Task required implementing these three capabilities while conforming to the NotificationAdapter interface.
parameters.rationale: Three-layer design: (1) RecipientResolver function type resolves identifiers to email addresses — default treats '@'-containing strings as emails, custom resolvers can look up user IDs. (2) Template rendering via a TEMPLATES record keyed by NotificationType — each template produces subject, HTML, and plain-text output with HTML escaping for XSS safety. (3) EmailTransport interface abstracts delivery — InMemoryTransport for dev/test, swap for real providers. This keeps the adapter testable and extensible without changing the NotificationAdapter contract.
parameters.summary: Email adapter implemented with template rendering, recipient resolution, and pluggable transport

parameters.detail: Replaced EmailAdapter stub with full implementation. Three key features: (1) Template rendering — TEMPLATES record maps NotificationType to HTML+text email bodies with XSS-safe escaping. (2) Recipient resolution — RecipientResolver function type, default resolves '@'-containing strings, custom resolvers for user ID lookup. (3) Pluggable EmailTransport interface — InMemoryTransport for dev/test, swap for real SMTP/SES/SendGrid. Added 22 tests covering send, recipient resolution, template rendering for all 4 types, HTML escaping, custom transport, and transport error handling. All 93 tests pass, TypeScript compiles clean. The adapter conforms to NotificationAdapter interface and is already wired into NotificationService via registerAdapter().
parameters.summary: Agent-2: Email adapter fully implemented — template rendering, recipient resolution, pluggable transport, 22 unit tests
